title: Spicy Gooey Vietnamese Chicken Pho-Ga
description: A spicy and flavorful Vietnamese-inspired dish featuring grilled chicken, katsuobushi, and a rich broth. Perfect for meal prep and suitable for a Whole30 diet.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and black pepper.
Grill the chicken for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
In a large pot, combine the fish stock and coconut milk.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and add the katsuobushi, fish sauce, and sambal oelek.
Simmer the broth for 10-15 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
Add the grilled chicken to the pot and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper.
Serve the chicken and broth hot, garnished with green onions, lime wedges, and fresh cilantro.
Refrigerate or freeze the broth and chicken for later use.
